page: Microsoft Azure D48nlds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Dnldv6 server family. It is powered by an Intel processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server features 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware is designed with a network-optimized profile, trading off memory capacity for compute density and local disk space.

The low memory-to-core ratio of 1:2 represents a tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for memory-intensive databases. However, the combination of 48 dedicated cores and substantial local storage makes it appropriate for network-heavy workloads, distributed file systems, and compute-intensive applications with moderate memory requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure D48nlds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dnldv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, establishing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. For storage, the instance is equipped with 2835 GB of local disk and supports Premium Storage. Designed as a network-optimized and low-memory virtual machine, it trades off RAM capacity for compute density and local storage space. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the low-memory configuration provides a more economical option for compute-heavy and storage-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints.

tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine combining forty-eight dedicated compute cores with substantial local storage and a low memory-to-core ratio.

96.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
